summary: Every decision will be judged from the point of view of the human running the desk, not from random module combinations.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The desk scan starts with time-of-day, day type, event proximity, overnight handoff, and what just happened before any playbook is considered.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The desk will classify beta, vol, rates, FX, sector leadership, breadth, and options state before looking for trades.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Options surface state, dealer pressure, skew, gamma, open-interest clustering, and flow context are first-class inputs, not optional garnish.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The desk decides posture first: flat, cautious, probing, normal, pressing, reduce-only, or no-overnight.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Only playbooks that are relevant to the current state become eligible. The full stack is never active by default.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Instrument choice, strike choice, sizing, and execution shape come after permission and posture are established.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Every decision path requires review and explanation output, not just a score. The operator must be able to see why the system acted or refused to act.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The operator-facing screen will be a bounded cockpit of roughly 30 live signals, while the backend may compute far more state.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

canonical_name: Session Clock as First-Class Feature Family
summary: The trading day is not homogeneous. Session phase changes meaning, weighting, and eligibility of modules.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The system separates market-state detection from setup classification, expression recommendation, and execution method.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Overnight exposure is its own planning artefact influenced by signal cleanliness, options surface shape, gap risk, and human monitoring constraints.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Replay is an evidence tool that asks what was known at the time, which modules were eligible, and what the system would have done then.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The desk runs bounded ensembles of modules and playbooks. No single signal owns the whole system.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

canonical_name: Adversarial Markets and Edge Decay Monitor
summary: The system preserves the idea that markets deceive, edges decay, and a monitor for false positives and edge erosion belongs in the design.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Qualitative and narrative context may re-enter only as constrained typed input, never as unbounded prose inside runtime logic.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: No-trade states and deliberate sitting out are adult desk behaviour and remain first-class outcomes.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: The architecture will preserve no-trade and veto outcomes as legitimate outputs, not as failures to find action.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Narrative desk interpretation and deterministic execution logic remain separated into a two-brain architecture.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

canonical_name: Existing Inventory Versus Fresh Deployable Capital
summary: The system must distinguish inventory already in market from fresh capital still allowed to deploy.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

canonical_name: Beta Tape Uses ES and NQ First
summary: US index futures are primary beta discovery instruments and belong in the raw state model ahead of cash ETF proxies.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

canonical_name: Asia Handoff Is a Structured Layer
summary: Japan and Hong Kong are structured Asia risk inputs that feed overnight repricing and pre-open conviction, not random extra tickers.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Volatility is a complex including short-dated vol, 30-day vol, tech-specific vol, vol-of-vol, skew, and term structure.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Breadth and concentration belong in the state model because peers alone do not explain whether a move is broad or narrow.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Price and volume are raw inputs. VWAP and anchored VWAP are derived execution-state metrics and will not be treated as raw capture.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.

summary: Every future code, contract, and architecture decision will be judged through the lens of the human operating the desk.
notes: Recovered from desk-cognition and legacy doctrine documents.
